title: Lehtimaki and others (Respondents) v Cooper (Appellant)

metadata.title: Lehtimaki and others (Respondents) v Cooper (Appellant)
metadata.summary: Principle issue: Does the court have jurisdiction to direct members of a charitable company on how to exercise their powers absent a breach of fiduciary duty?
extracts: Issue 3: Does section 217 of the Companies Act 2006 allow the Court to direct a member to exercise his discretion in a particular way when Parliament has provided for members to pass the resolution?............. 50 The range of the arguments on this issue........................................................................................................ 50 My conclusions on the third issue ................................................................................................................... 51

content: {"title":"Lehtimaki and others (Respondents) v Cooper (Appellant)","citation":"UKSC/2018/0150","url":"https://supremecourt.uk/cases/uksc-2018-0150","summary":"Principle issue: Does the court have jurisdiction to direct members of a charitable company on how to exercise their powers absent a breach of fiduciary duty?","extracts":["Issue 2: Have circumstances arisen with respect to the section 217 resolution in which the Court can exercise its jurisdiction over fiduciaries in relation to Dr Lehtimäki?......................................................................... 37 The competing positions of the parties ........................................................................................................... 37 My conclusion on this issue ............................................................................................................................. 39","Issue 3: Does section 217 of the Companies Act 2006 allow the Court to direct a member to exercise his discretion in a particular way when Parliament has provided for members to pass the resolution?............. 50 The range of the arguments on this issue........................................................................................................ 50 My conclusions on the third issue ................................................................................................................... 51"],"category":"procedure","score":1,"title_key":"lehtimaki and others v cooper"}
